explanation: The property states that every time prop_0 is true, prop_1 should be true immediately (without any delay) at the same time.

The statement says that whenever prop_0 is true, prop_1 must occur immediately (without delay) in response. In LTL, this is expressed by requiring that globally, whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 holds in the same state. This is written as G (prop_0 -> prop_1).

The requirement specifies that whenever prop_0 is true at any point in time, then prop_1 should be true immediately (without delay) in response—this means that everywhere (globally), it is not possible for prop_0 to be true and prop_1 not to be true at the same moment, i.e., prop_0 implies prop_1, globally.

The property states that whenever prop_0 is true, prop_1 must be true at the same moment, i.e., without any delay. In LTL, this is typically captured by the invariant that (prop_0 -> prop_1) must hold globally.
This requirement expresses that whenever prop_0 is true, prop_1 must be true at the same time step (no delay). That is, prop_0 implies prop_1 globally.

The formula expresses that every time prop_0 becomes true, prop_1 must also be true at the same instant (no delay). This is captured by the property G (prop_0 -> prop_1), which states that globally, if prop_0 holds, then prop_1 holds at the same moment.

The formula says that whenever 'prop_0' holds, 'prop_1' must immediately hold in the same state, i.e., there is no delay. In LTL, this can be directly captured by stating that globally, 'prop_0' implies 'prop_1'.
This formula says that globally, whenever prop_0 holds, then prop_1 must also hold at the same time (no delay), i.e., immediately. This is captured by G (prop_0 -> prop_1).

The phrase 'whenever prop_0 then in response prop_1 without any delay' means that every time prop_0 is true, prop_1 must immediately be true at the same time (zero delay). In LTL, this is captured by stating that globally, whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold in that state.
The statement means that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold in the same state (immediately, without any delay). In LTL, this is captured by saying 'globally, if prop_0 then prop_1'.
